title: cjk-ctex
description: A Chinese XeTeX document via \documentclass{ctexart} with NO host font supplied — it falls back to the BUNDLED fandol font (§6.3 exception). Because the §5.4(a) scan reads \usepackage/\RequirePackage and NOT \documentclass, ctexart is INVISIBLE to it: pass 1 fails 'ctexart.cls not found' against core alone (streamed LIVE), the §5.4(b) missing-file retry mounts academic, and the re-run compiles. This drives the retry path INDEPENDENTLY of the scan (the sci-paper covers the scan). The Chinese is verified STRUCTURALLY (no pixel comparison, §8): the embedded FandolSong CID subset + its embedded font program + the CID glyph run prove the Chinese rendered with fandol — the CJK subset carries no ToUnicode CMap, so recoverText reconstructs only the Latin runs (WasmTeX/fandol), which is why the text snippets are the Latin words and the Chinese is asserted via the font/glyph structure.
